title: Multicenter iEEG dataset for classification of graphoelements and artifactual signals (MAYO)
description: A multicenter intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) dataset acquired from epilepsy patients at Mayo Clinic for classification of graphoelements and artifactual signals. The dataset comprises approximately 2 hours of interictal recordings from the first night post-implant, acquired using Neuralynx Cheetah systems and downsampled to 5 kHz. This BIDS-formatted release includes standardized metadata, electrode localization information, and seizure onset zone annotations to support machine learning and signal processing research in epilepsy.
methods_description: Intracranial recordings were acquired using Neuralynx Cheetah systems at 32 kHz and downsampled to 5 kHz with 60 Hz power line frequency. Depth and grid/strip electrodes (AD-Tech/PMT, Platinum/Iridium) with 5/10 mm spacing were implanted. Approximately 2 hours of interictal recordings were collected during the first night post-implant (01:00-03:00). Ground electrode was placed at scalp Fcz.
